data.title: Coinbase Acquires Deribit in $2.9B Mega Deal to Dominate Crypto Derivatives
data.description:
TL;DR Coinbase acquired Deribit for $2. 9 billion in the largest deal in the crypto industry to date, combining cash and company stock. The acquisition will boost its global presence and strengthen its institutional offering in a derivatives market with growing demand. Deribit dominated BTC and ETH options trading with $1. 2 trillion in volume in 2024. . . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/coinbase-acquires-deribit-in-2-9b-mega-deal-to-dominate-crypto-derivatives/

data.title: Trump calls Fed chair Jerome Powell ‘the fool I like very much’
data.description:
President Trump went off on Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell yet again on Thursday from the White House and didn’t even try to hide how he felt. In a post on Truth Social, the president wrote, “’Too Late’ Jerome Powell is a FOOL, who doesn’t have a clue. Other than that, I like him very […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-calls-powell-the-fool-i-like-very-much/

data.title: XRP Price Repeating History? 2017-Like Rally To Send Price To $10
data.description:
Crypto analyst ArShevelev has raised the possibility of history repeating itself for the XRP price. If so, the analyst indicated that the altcoin could witness a 2017-like rally, which would send its price to double digits. XRP Price To Reach $10 If History Repeats Itself In a TradingView post, ArShevelev predicted that the XRP price could reach as high as $10 if history repeats itself. He remarked that the current XRP chart screams “déjà vu” with the altcoin’s price action mirroring the 2014 to 2018 cycle
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/xrp-repeating-2017-like-rally/

data.title: XRP Open Interest Climbs as Ripple Launches Community Rewards Drive
data.description:
XRP’s price activity and community engagement have shown a sharp increase as traders boost their positions and Ripple unveils a new community rewards initiative. While short-term speculation increases, industry observers caution against long-term price expectations that may not align with the broader economic framework.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/05/08/xrp-open-interest-climbs-as-ripple-launches-community-rewards-drive/

data.title: Missouri House has passed a bill to exempt capital gains tax on crypto transactions
data.description:
Missouri has obtained final approval for legislation that will suspend the personal capital gains tax this year. Missouri will become the first state in the United States to eliminate the capital gains tax on income from the sale of stocks, real estate, and crypto assets. The bill passed in the Senate last month and then […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/missouri-has-passed-a-bill-on-crypto-tax/

data.title: USDC Treasury Burns Over 50 Million Tokens on Solana
data.description: According to BlockBeats, WhaleAlert has reported that approximately 50.59 million USDC tokens were destroyed by the USDC Treasury on the Solana blockchain. This transaction was detected 17 minutes prior to the report.

data.title: Spyware Firm Ordered To Pay $167,250,000 in Damages for Enabling WhatsApp Hacks of 1,400 Smartphone Devices: Report
data.description:
An Israel-based cyber intelligence firm is reportedly ordered to pay damages for the security breach of the instant messaging platform WhatsApp six years ago. The NSO Group is behind the zero-click spyware Pegasus, which allows hackers to infiltrate smartphones without requiring user interaction, such as clicking a link or opening a message.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/05/08/spyware-firm-ordered-to-pay-167250000-in-damages-for-enabling-whatsapp-hacks-of-1400-smartphone-devices-report/

data.title: Are layer 2s good for Ethereum, or are they ‘extractive?’
data.description:
Layer 2s have been a great blockchain success story. They’ve reduced congestion on the Ethereum mainnet, driving down gas fees while preserving security. But maybe they’ve become too successful, drawing chain activity and fee income from the parent that spawned them? At least that’s what some are suggesting lately, most recently at Cornell Tech’s blockchain conference in late April. Indeed, some think Ethereum should be a little greedier, or at least fight harder for a bigger part of the revenue pie, particularly sequencing fees
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/layer-2s-good-for-ethereum-or-extractive?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Social Media Platform X Receives Indian Government Order to Block Accounts
data.description: According to BlockBeats, social media platform X has received an executive order from the Indian government. The order mandates the platform to block over 8,000 accounts within India.
